concept: Caption / Present Timed Text for Speech and Meaningful Sound
description: To present synchronized text or another readable display for speech and meaningful sound in a live or recorded event. Captions can identify the speaker and report relevant non-speech sound. 'sitheku' (transcribe) preserves verbal content in writing without requiring synchronization.
sound_symbolism: Dental breath opens 'the' quietly. Stressed 'ka' interrupts it with a crisp velar closure and broad 'a', then final 'si' returns to fine air and bright 'i'. A breath, a firm center, and a hiss give the word three sharply separated beats.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'lo mia ha haolu thekasi.' (We caption this speech.) The event-noun rule supplies captions or captioning according to context. The clause may name whether the event is live, recorded, complete, delayed, or edited.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A community meeting can change its communication practice without requiring every participant to use the same sense.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Captions let speech remain available when hearing it is not, and they expose whether laughter, alarms, or speaker identity carried meaning that words alone missed.

semantic_domains.communication: speech and meaningful sound presented in a readable form
semantic_domains.creation: timed readable text made for a sounding event
semantic_domains.temporal: text synchronized with a live or recorded event
